title: onobrychis laxiflora
intro: Onobrychis laxiflora, also known as Persian clover or narrowleaf bird's foot trefoil, is a type of perennial legume native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Onobrychis laxiflora include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Onobrychis laxiflora can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to make tinctures and ointments.
